trivia: The last McDonnell Douglas widebody: a stretched, winglet-wearing DC-10 successor with a two-crew cockpit. Passenger service has ended; the type identity survives through its famous freighter descendants.
The Dex records this passenger identity separately from the MD-11F, whose big cargo fleet is its own future entry.
funFacts: The last trijet airliner built in the West.
